title: Avalanche ETF breaks a massive daily inflow record
content:
A specific corner of the crypto market is seeing a sudden spike in institutional interest.

The VanEck Avalanche ETF (Nasdaq: VAVX) recently recorded its highest single-day inflow since its debut. According to data from mid-February shared on AVAX One's official X handle, the fund attracted $4.26 million in a single day.

This surge comes despite a dip in the underlying asset's price, creating an interesting divergence between market value and investor behavior.

Related: Avalanche ETF debuts on Wall Street

Understanding the VAVX fund

On Jan. 26, the VanEck Avalanche ETF debuted on Wall Street. It stands out as the first and only ETF designed to offer investors exposure to both the price return and the staking rewards of AVAX.

To attract capital, VanEck is currently waiving all sponsor fees. This waiver applies to the fund’s first $500 million in assets or lasts until Feb. 28, whichever comes first. Once that threshold is crossed, the firm states the sponsor fee will be 0.20%.

VanEck describes the goal as offering efficient and cost-effective exposure to one of the industry's "most robust blockchain ecosystems."

Related: Tokenized Treasuries are like digital savings accounts, says Ava Labs VP

A look at market reaction and pricing

Despite the record inflows recorded last week, the price of the asset itself has struggled. At the time of writing, the AVAX token was trading at $9.29, down 2.3% in 24 hours. Similarly, VAVX was trading at $19.27 at press time, representing a 3.6% decline.

Avalanche currently holds a market cap of $4 million approximately, ranking it as the 24th largest cryptocurrency. However, the asset is significantly down from its peak. AVAX has seen its price fall more than 94% from its all-time high.

Most popular on TheStreet Roundtable:

'Black Swan' author warns investors to brace for bankruptcies America’s most shorted stock is Wall Street’s Bitcoin machine Trump targets tech giants with a massive new energy rule

What is Avalanche?

For those new to the space, Avalanche is a blockchain network developed by Ava Labs and launched in 2020. It was created by a team of developers including Stephen Buttolph, Amani Moin, Kevin Sekniqi, and Emin Gün Sirer.

The platform is a layer-1 network, meaning it provides the base infrastructure for other applications to run. It offers a fast, low-cost environment for building decentralized applications (dApps).

Like Ethereum and Solana, the Avalanche blockchain network uses a proof-of-stake (PoS) consensus mechanism to verify and add transactions.

Ava Labs described the ETF launch as a meaningful step in expanding institutional access to the Avalanche network, highlighting growing recognition of the blockchain for real-world applications across enterprises and governments.

What's next

Looking back, AVAX was once viewed as a rival to Ethereum in 2021. While it experienced a notable breakout in mid-2025—surging approximately 30% over a 30-day period and outpacing Bitcoin—the long-term recovery remains uncertain.

By 2026, the question remains whether any catalysts can help this altcoin stage a comeback. It is difficult to predict what AVAX price will reach because the cryptocurrency market is volatile and subject to purely speculative pricing.

Related: Why decentralized apps matter: Ava Labs Exec explains the real difference

title: VanEck's new Avalanche ETF filing to include staking rewards for AVAX investors
content:
VanEck has updated its filing for an Avalanche exchange-traded fund, VAVX, to include staking rewards and generate income for investors.

In an amended S-1 filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission, the firm disclosed that the fund may stake up to 70% of its AVAX holdings to generate yield, with Coinbase Crypto Services listed as the initial staking provider.

Prediction Market powered by

Any rewards, minus a 4% service fee from Coinbase, would accrue to the fund and be reflected in the ETF’s net asset value.

Under the plan, AVAX will be held with regulated custodians, including Anchorage Digital and Coinbase Custody, both of which store tokens offline in cold wallets.

The fund will not utilize leverage or derivatives, and it will track AVAX’s price through the MarketVector Avalanche Benchmark Rate, a custom index constructed from major exchanges.

If approved, the fund would trade under the ticker VAVX on Nasdaq. Bitwise last month updated its spot Avalanche ETF filing with the SEC to also enable yield generation.

Read more: U.S. Clears Way for Crypto ETPs to Get Into Yield Without Triggering Tax Problems
